core_payload.content:
# Symphony Assignment Intake

## 1. Role
Symphony Assignment Intake owns the work-entry surfaces that feed the orchestrator. It keeps tracker demand, workspace entry, swarm-fed assignments, and capsule-job intake together so execution begins from explicit sources instead of from a hidden scheduler fog.

## 2. Assignment Intake Surface
This sub-hub groups `tracker`, `workspace`, `n-infinity`, and `capsule-job`. Together they define how issue work, graph jobs, workspace-origin requests, and swarm-driven assignments become bounded execution candidates before a live session is opened.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own daemon continuity, workflow safety posture, or retry and observability state. Those responsibilities belong to `live-session-fabric`, `workflow-safety`, and `run-observability`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Assignment Intake groups the work-entry surfaces of the orchestration system: Tracker, Workspace, N-Infinity, and Capsule Job. It keeps issue demand, workspace-origin requests, swarm-fed assignments, and graph-job intake together so Symphony starts from explicit assignment sources instead of a hidden scheduler fog. This boundary matters because orchestration quality depends on understanding where work came from before any daemon, session, or retry machinery begins to act. It gives Symphony one legible intake seam for repository and capsule-driven execution.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Symphony decomposition to isolate tracker, workspace, swarm, and capsule-job entry surfaces from live session fabric and run-state handling.
